ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers moussaka as a composite dish; chicken variant is not separately coded. Closest defensible match applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of chicken moussaka is approximately 400 g, providing around 650 kcal, suitable as a complete main meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Moussaka, with meat (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Turkish-style chicken moussaka exists in USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy moussaka composite entries and cross-referenced with USDA chicken mince, eggplant, potato, and béchamel component data. Input AI-generated values (confidence 0.7) were reviewed against USDA component data and retained where consistent. Energy recalculated from macros: protein 11.25g × 4 + fat 10.0g × 9 + carbohydrate 8.75g × 4 = 45 + 90 + 35 = 170 kcal; input value of 162.5 kcal is within plausible range given fibre contribution; 162.5 kcal retained as provided.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Turkish chicken moussaka. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed baked meat-and-vegetable dishes with béchamel from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish contains moderate carbohydrate (8.75g/100g) primarily from potato and béchamel flour, partially offset by high protein (11.25g/100g) and fat (10.0g/100g) which slow gastric emptying. GL per serving = GI/100 × carbohydrate_g per serving = 45/100 × 35g (8.75g × 400g/100) = 15.75.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 225 mg/100g; estimated 900 mg per 400g serving
hypertension.guidance: A 400g serving delivers approximately 900 mg sodium — 39% of the 2300 mg daily limit — so individuals with hypertension should request reduced added salt in the béchamel and seasoning, and consider a smaller portion of 200–250g to keep sodium intake below 500 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate 8.75g/100g with low GI (~45); high protein 11.25g/100g moderates postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low estimated GI (~45) and high protein content (11.25g/100g) from chicken mince slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose response; however, a 400g serving delivers approximately 35g carbohydrate, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 200–250g and request a thinner béchamel layer to reduce refined-flour carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.75g/100g and cholesterol 37.5mg/100g from béchamel and chicken; trans fat 0.13g/100g present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 400g serving provides approximately 15g saturated fat and 150mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request a lighter béchamel topping, choose lean chicken mince, and limit portion size to 200g to keep saturated fat intake below 7g per meal in line with AHA dietary guidance.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetables (eggplant, potato, tomato) contain solanine which may aggravate joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should avoid or substitute eggplant, potato, and tomato with zucchini or sweet potato, and monitor for joint flare after consumption; olive oil and oregano in the dish provide beneficial anti-inflammatory components.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Moussaka (Turkish: Mousakka Tavuk) is a Turkish-style baked layered dish consisting of seasoned ground chicken mince, sliced eggplant, and potato, topped with a creamy béchamel sauce made from flour, milk, and egg, then baked until golden and set. Unlike the Greek lamb-based moussaka, the Turkish variant commonly uses chicken and may incorporate cumin, oregano, onion, garlic, and tomato in the meat layer. The dish has a rich, savoury flavour with a soft, layered texture — tender vegetables and moist chicken beneath a smooth, lightly browned béchamel crust. Per 100g, it provides approximately 162.5 kcal, 11.25g protein, 10.0g fat (3.75g saturated), 8.75g carbohydrate, 1.75g dietary fibre, and 225mg sodium. The estimated glycemic index is low (~45), making it a moderate-glycaemic meal when portioned appropriately. Clinically, the high protein content slows gastric emptying and moderates postprandial glucose response, beneficial for Type 2 Diabetes management; however, sodium content and saturated fat from béchamel require portion control for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. The dish contains nightshade vegetables (eggplant, potato, tomato) which may aggravate rheumatoid arthritis in sensitive individuals. It is halal-certified in Singapore restaurant contexts and commonly served as a main meal in Turkish and Middle Eastern restaurants. A standard plate serving is approximately 400g.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken moussaka?
What is the difference between Greek and Turkish moussaka?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Turkish chicken moussaka exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central moussaka composite and component data, cross-referenced with the AI-generated input values (confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Potassium value (340mg/100g) is estimated from eggplant, potato, and tomato component data in USDA FoodData Central and retained from input reasoning.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Turkish chicken moussaka composite not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish not reported in available approved sources. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data for this specific composite dish not available in USDA FoodData Central for the Turkish chicken moussaka preparation as of 2024. trans_fat_g of 0.13g/100g is retained from input; this is plausible for a dish containing béchamel sauce made with dairy. Potassium 340mg/100g is an estimate derived from USDA component data for eggplant, potato, and tomato contributions. All core macros (protein_g 11.25, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 8.75) are non-zero and nutritionally consistent with a baked chicken and vegetable dish with béchamel sauce. Input ethnic_tags included 'Kebab' and 'Shawarma' which are not applicable to moussaka and have been excluded from this record. The dish is sold via GrabFood in Singapore and is classified as halal based on input dietary_tags.
